questions.question: Find the bug in this phishing email HTML that would alert a security-conscious user.
questions.codeSnippet:
Dear User,

Your account has been limited. Click here to restore access.

Thank you,
PayPal Security
questions.explanation: The bug is on line 3: the link text displays 'http://paypal.com' but the actual href points to 'verify-id3245.com'. This mismatched anchor text is a major red flag that should be checked by hovering.

questions.question: Find the operational security (OPSEC) flaw in this vishing script.
questions.codeSnippet:
Attacker: Hi, this is Mark from IT. I'm calling from my personal cell because the office phones are down.
Target: Okay... what's this about?
Attacker: We need to verify your VPN token. Can you read me the 6-digit code?
questions.explanation: The bug is on line 0: 'calling from my personal cell' is a major red flag. Legitimate IT would never use a personal phone, and it provides a traceable number.

questions.question: Identify the mistake in this physical tailgating attempt.
questions.codeSnippet:
Attacker approaches secure door carrying a large box.
Employee badges in and holds door.
Attacker: 'Thanks, man. I work on the 5th floor.'
Employee: 'Sure, no problem.'
questions.explanation: The bug is on line 2: voluntarily stating 'I work on the 5th floor' is unnecessary detail that could be contradicted later. A simple 'Thanks' is sufficient.

questions.question: Find the error in this caller ID spoofing configuration.
questions.codeSnippet:
SIP INVITE message:
From: "IT Help Desk" <sip:5551234567@attacker-ip>
P-Asserted-Identity: "IT Help Desk" <sip:5551234567@attacker-ip>
Remote-Party-ID: <sip:anonymous@anonymous.invalid>
questions.explanation: The bug is on line 2: the Remote-Party-ID is set to anonymous, which may cause the target's phone to display 'Unknown' instead of the spoofed number.

questions.question: What's wrong with this USB baiting label strategy?
questions.codeSnippet:
USB drive label: 'CONFIDENTIAL: Q4 Layoff Plan'
Placement: In the company cafeteria, on a table.
questions.explanation: The bug is on line 1: 'CONFIDENTIAL' labels may make employees more cautious, and a cafeteria table is high-traffic but might be cleaned. Better label: 'HR Staffing Review' and placement near HR offices.

questions.question: Find the social engineering flaw in this email subject line and body combination.
questions.codeSnippet:
Subject: URGENT!!! Your account will be DELETED in 24 hours!!!
Body: Dear user, click this link immediately to avoid permanent loss of access to all corporate systems.
questions.explanation: The bug is on line 0: excessive exclamation marks, all-caps, and extreme urgency are classic spam triggers that email filters detect and users have been trained to ignore.

questions.question: Identify the mistake in this vishing call handling a challenge.
questions.codeSnippet:
Target: 'Wait, what's your name again? Who's your manager?'
Attacker: 'I'm the Senior IT Director, and I don't answer to you. Now give me the code.'
questions.explanation: The bug is on line 1: becoming aggressive and pulling rank when challenged is a huge red flag. A better response is calm deflection or offering to call back.
